name: Germany
contextBlurb: Germany regulates stablecoins under the EU Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) alongside national laws (KWG, ZAG). BaFin is the lead supervisor. MiCA treats e-money tokens (EMTs), such as euro-backed stablecoins, as electronic money requiring authorisation and strong consumer-protection, reserve, and transparency rules. Asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) are also permitted with MiCA authorisation and prudential conditions. Algorithmic or unbacked designs are effectively excluded from the MiCA issuance perimeter.

legislation.title: Apr 2026 ESMA confirmed that the MiCA transitional period ends mid-2026; crypto firms must hold a full MiCA/CASP licence by that deadline. As of April 2026, 199 CASPs are licensed across the EU; no Asset-Referenced Token (ART) issuer has yet been approved.
AllUnity (DWS/Deutsche Bank partners) introduces a BaFin-regulated euro stablecoin initiative (EURAU)
BaFin orders wind-up of Ethena GmbH's USDe business in Germany (order dated Apr 14; notice published Apr 15)
BaFin prohibits new USDe offerings to the public and freezes reserves; special representative appointed
CASP rules applicable (with national transitional choices)
MiCA published in the Official Journal

keyFigures.name: BaFin (Federal Financial Supervisory Authority)
keyFigures.role: National competent authority for MiCA authorisations and supervision (issuers and CASPs), and for ZAG/KWG compliance.

keyFigures.name: Deutsche Bundesbank
keyFigures.role: Cooperates with BaFin on supervision; macroprudential and financial-stability analysis; member of the Financial Stabilit

news.headline: Digital Watch – ESMA confirms end of MiCA transition period (April 2026)

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Germany regulates stablecoins under the EU Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) alongside national laws (KWG, ZAG). BaFin is the lead supervisor. MiCA treats e-money tokens (EMTs), such as euro-backed stablecoins, as electronic money requiring authorisation and strong consumer-protection, reserve, and transparency rules. Asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) are also permitted with MiCA authorisation and prudential conditions. Algorithmic or unbacked designs are effectively excluded from the MiCA issuance perimeter.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: E-money when qualifying as an EMT under MiCA/ZAG; otherwise a crypto-asset/financial instrument under KWG, depending on structure.

regulators.name: BaFin
